title: gmelina delavayana
intro: Gmelina delavayana, also known as the Delavay tree or Yulan, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to Yunnan, China, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory diseases such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's leaves and bark have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are believed to offer various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the plant's leaves have been used to treat digestive issues and as a natural remedy for skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its antimicrobial and antifungal properties.
